199	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
200	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
201	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
202	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
203	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
204	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
205	
206	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
207	
208	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
209	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
210	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
211	
212	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
213	
214	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
215	
216	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
217	{
218	  "schema_version": 1,
219	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
220	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
221	  "per_ac": [
222	    { "number": 1, "item": "acceptance criterion text", "grade": "pass", "evidence": "file:line or test evidence" }
223	  ],
224	  "findings": [
225	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
226	  ]
227	}
228	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
229	
230	Rules:
231	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK".
232	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
233	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
234	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`
235	
236	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
237	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
238	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
239	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
240		Caps evidence.Caps
241	}

251	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
252	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {

258	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
259	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
260	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
261	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
262		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
263	}
264	
265	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
266	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
267	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
268	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
269		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
270	}

272	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
273	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
274	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
275	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
276	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
277	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
278	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
279	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
280		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
281	}

284	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
285	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {

289	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
290		caps := opts.Caps
291		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
292			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
293		}
294	
295		var sb strings.Builder
296		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
297	
298		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
299	
300		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
301		beadStart := sb.Len()
302		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
303		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
304	
305		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
306			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
307		} else {
308			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
309		}
310	
311		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
312			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
313		} else {
314			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
315		}
316	
317		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
318			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
319		}
320	
321		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
322			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
323		}
324	
325		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
326		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
327			Name:          "bead",
328			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
329			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
330		})
331	
332		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
333		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
334		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
335			invStart := sb.Len()
336			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
337			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
338			for _, f := range allFiles {
339				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
340				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
341			}
342			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
343			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
344				Name:          "changed-files",
345				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
346				SelectedItems: paths,
347			})
348		}
349	
350		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
351		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
352		if len(refs) == 0 {
353			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
354		} else {
355			for _, ref := range refs {
356				if ref.Title != "" {
357					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
358				} else {
359					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
360				}
361				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
362				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
363				if readErr != nil {
364					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
365					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
366						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
367						TruncationReason: "read_error",
368						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
369					})
370				} else {
371					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
372					if truncated {
373						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
374					}
375					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
376					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
377						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
378						BytesIncluded: len(content),
379						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
380					}
381					if truncated {
382						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
383						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
384					}
385					sections = append(sections, sec)
386				}
387				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
388			}
389		}
390		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
391	
392		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
393		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
394		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
395		diffSection.Name = "diff"
396		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
397		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
398	
399		if includeProse {
400			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
401			if proseErr != nil {
402				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
403				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
404				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
405				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
406					Name:          "prose-review",
407					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
408				})
409			} else {
410				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
411				sb.WriteString("\n")
412				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
413			}
414		}
415	
416		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
417		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
418		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
419		if includeProse {
420			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
421		}
422		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
423	
424		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
425	
426		out := sb.String()
427		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
428			Name:    "review-prompt",
429			Content: out,
430		}}, len(out))
431		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
432			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
433			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
434			Sections: sections,
435		}
436	}

438	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
439		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
440		if err != nil {
441			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
442		}
443	
444		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
445		if err != nil {
446			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
447		}
448		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
449		if err != nil {
450			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
451		}
452	
453		var src strings.Builder
454		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
455			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
456		}
457		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
458			src.WriteString(desc)
459			src.WriteString("\n\n")
460		}
461		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
462			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
463			src.WriteString(acc)
464			src.WriteString("\n\n")
465		}
466		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
467			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
468			src.WriteString(notes)
469			src.WriteString("\n")
470		}
471	
472		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
473		var sb strings.Builder
474		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
475		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
476		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
477		if len(findings) == 0 {
478			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
479		} else {
480			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
481			for _, finding := range findings {
482				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
483				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
484				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
485				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
486			}
487			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
488		}
489		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
490	
491		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
492			Name:          "prose-review",
493			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
494		}
495		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
496		return sb.String(), section, nil
497	}

499	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
500	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
501	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
502	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
503	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
504	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
505	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
506		if len(files) == 0 {
507			return originalDiff
508		}
509		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
510			if f.Path == "" {
511				return 3
512			}
513			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
514				return 0
515			}
516			for _, r := range refs {
517				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
518					return 1
519				}
520			}
521			return 2
522		}
523		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
524		copy(ordered, files)
525		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
526	
527		var b2 strings.Builder
528		for _, f := range ordered {
529			body := f.Body
530			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
531				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
532			}
533			b2.WriteString(body)
534		}
535		return b2.String()

538	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
539	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
540	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
541	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
542	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
543		var b strings.Builder
544		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
545		b.WriteString(f.Path)
546		b.WriteString(" b/")
547		b.WriteString(f.Path)
548		b.WriteString("\n")
549		if f.Stat != "" {
550			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
551			b.WriteString("\n")
552		}
553		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
554			b.WriteString(h)
555			b.WriteString("\n")
556		}
557		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
558		return b.String()
559	}

561	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
562	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
563	// exact symbolic resolution).
564	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
565		if text == "" || p == "" {
566			return false
567		}
568		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
569			return true
570		}
571		base := path.Base(p)
572		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
573	}

575	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
576	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
577	// documents.
578	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
579		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
580			return false
581		}
582		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
583		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
584			return true
585		}
586		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
587			return true
588		}
589		return false
590	}

593	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {

595		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
596			"&", "&",
597			"<", "<",
598			">", ">",
599		).Replace(s))
600		return buf.String()

636	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
637	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
638	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
639	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
640	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
641	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
642	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
643	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
644		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
645	}

647	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
648	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
649	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
650	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
651	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
652	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
653		correlation := map[string]string{}
654		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
655			correlation[k] = v
656		}
657		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
658		if meta.GroupID != "" {
659			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
660			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
661		}
662		if impl.Harness != "" {
663			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
664		}
665		if impl.Provider != "" {
666			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
667		}
668		if impl.Model != "" {
669			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
670		}
671		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
672			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
673		}
674		minPower := 0
675		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
676			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
677		}
678		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
679		return AgentRunRuntime{
680			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
681			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
682			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
683			Correlation:         correlation,
684			Role:                "reviewer",
685			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
686			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
687			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
688			ClearProfile:        true,
689			ClearMaxPower:       true,
690		}
691	}

697	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) reviewerDispatchProfile {
698		floor := 0
699		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
700			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
701		}
702		if r.Runner != nil {
703			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
704		}
705		selectedSvc := r.Service
706		if selectedSvc == nil {
707			factory := serviceRunFactory
708			if factory == nil {
709				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
710			}
711			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
712			if err != nil {
713				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
714			}
715			selectedSvc = built
716		}
717		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
718		if err != nil {
719			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
720		}
721		name := ""
722		if floor > 0 {
723			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, floor)
724		} else {
725			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
726		}
727		if name == "" {
728			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
729		}
730		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
731			if profile.Name == name {
732				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: profile.MinPower}
733			}
734		}
735		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: floor}
736	}

740			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
741				return profile.Name
742			}
743		}
744		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
745	}

762	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
763		if len(correlation) == 0 {
764			return ""
765		}
766		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
767		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
768			parts = append(parts, beadID)
769		}
770		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
771			parts = append(parts, groupID)
772		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
773			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
774		}
775		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
776			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
777		}
778		if len(parts) == 0 {
779			return ""
780		}
781		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
782	}

811	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
812	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
813	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.
814	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error) {
815		reviewer := *r
816		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
817			reviewer.ProjectRoot = projectRoot
818		}
819		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {
820			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
821		}
822		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
823		if beadID == "" {
824			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
825		}
826		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
827		if err != nil {
828			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
829		}
830		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
831		if workDir == "" {
832			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
833		}
834		impl := ImplementerRouting{
835			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
836			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
837			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
838			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
839			Correlation: map[string]string{
840				"bead_id":       beadID,
841				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
842				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
843				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
844				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
845				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
846			},
847		}
848		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir)
849		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
850		if review == nil {
851			if reduceErr != nil {
852				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
853			}
854			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
855		}
856		out := CandidateReviewResult{
857			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
858			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
859			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
860			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
861			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
862		}
863		if reduceErr != nil {
864			return out, reduceErr
865		}
866		return out, groupErr
867	}

869	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewBeadWithDiff(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting, diff, reviewWorkDir string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
870		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
871		if err != nil {
872			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
873		}
874	
875		// Resolve governing document references.
876		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
877	
878		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
879		iter := 1
880	
881		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
882		caps := r.Caps
883		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
884			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
885		}
886		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
887		prompt := built.Prompt
888		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
889		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
890		if err != nil {
891			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
892		}
893		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
894			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
895		}
896	
897		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
898		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
899		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
900		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
901		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
902		if built.Overflow {
903			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
904			reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl)
905			reviewRouteLabel := reviewProfile.Name
906			if r.Model != "" {
907				reviewRouteLabel = r.Model
908			} else if r.ModelRef != "" {
909				reviewRouteLabel = r.ModelRef
910			}
911			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
912				Sections:    built.Sections,
913				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
914				OutputBytes: 0,
915				Harness:     r.Harness,
916				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
917			}
918			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
919				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
920				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
921				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
922				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
923				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
924				BaseRev:         baseRev,
925				ResultRev:       resultRev,
926				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
927				CostUSD:         0,
928				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
929				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
930			}
931			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
932				Harness:          r.Harness,
933				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
934				BaseRev:          baseRev,
935				ResultRev:        resultRev,
936				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
937				BeadID:           beadID,
938				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
939				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
940			}, reviewArtifactResult{
941				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
942				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
943				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
944			})
945			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
946				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
947		}
948	
949		// Resolve reviewer routing. An explicit reviewer harness/model wins as an
950		// operator override; otherwise DDx sends only a fizeau profile hint plus
951		// the reviewer MinPower floor.
952		reviewHarness := r.Harness
953		reviewProfile := r.reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx, impl)
954	
955		start := time.Now()
956		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewProfile.Name)
957		if runRuntime.MinPowerOverride == 0 && reviewProfile.MinPower > 0 {
958			runRuntime.MinPowerOverride = reviewProfile.MinPower
959		}
960		reviewRouteLabel := r.applyExplicitReviewerPins(&runRuntime)
961		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
962		runRuntime.WorkDir = reviewWorkDir
963		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
964	
965		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
966		if runErr != nil {
967			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
968			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
969			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
970			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
971				Sections:    built.Sections,
972				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
973				OutputBytes: 0,
974				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
975				Harness:     reviewHarness,
976				Model:       reviewRouteLabel,
977			}
978			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
979				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
980				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
981				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
982				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
983				ReviewerModel:   reviewRouteLabel,
984				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
985				ResultRev:       resultRev,
986				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
987				DurationMS:      durationMS,
988				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
989				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
990				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
991			}
992			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
993				Harness:          reviewHarness,
994				Model:            reviewRouteLabel,
995				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
996				ResultRev:        resultRev,
997				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
998				BeadID:           beadID,
999				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1000				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1001			}, reviewArtifactResult{
1002				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
1003				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
1004				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
1005				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
1006			})
1007			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
1008		}
1009	
1010		actualHarness := reviewHarness
1011		actualModel := reviewRouteLabel
1012		actualProvider := ""
1013		actualPower := 0
1014		if result != nil {
1015			if result.Harness != "" {
1016				actualHarness = result.Harness
1017			}
1018			if result.Model != "" {
1019				actualModel = result.Model
1020			}
1021			actualProvider = result.Provider
1022			actualPower = result.ActualPower
1023			durationMS = result.DurationMS
1024		}
1025	
1026		output := ""
1027		sessionID := ""
1028		if result != nil {
1029			output = result.Output
1030			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
1031		}
1032		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
1033		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
1034		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
1035		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
1036		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
1037		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
1038		var strictVerdict Verdict
1039		var rationale string
1040		var findings []Finding
1041		if parseErr == nil {
1042			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
1043			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
1044			findings = parsed.Findings
1045			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
1046				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
1047				for _, f := range findings {
1048					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
1049					if line == "" {
1050						continue
1051					}
1052					if f.Location != "" {
1053						line = f.Location + ": " + line
1054					}
1055					parts = append(parts, line)
1056				}
1057				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1058			}
1059		}
1060		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
1061		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
1062			Sections:    built.Sections,
1063			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
1064			OutputBytes: len(output),
1065			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
1066			Harness:     actualHarness,
1067			Model:       actualModel,
1068		}
1069		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
1070			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
1071			Rationale:        rationale,
1072			PerAC:            parsed.PerAC,
1073			Findings:         findings,
1074			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
1075			RawOutput:        output,
1076			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
1077			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
1078			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
1079			SessionID:        sessionID,
1080			BaseRev:          baseRev,
1081			ResultRev:        resultRev,
1082			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
1083			DurationMS:       durationMS,
1084			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
1085			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
1086			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
1087		}
1088	
1089		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
1090		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
1091		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
1092		// regression. Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the
1093		// review outcome itself.
1094		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
1095			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
1096				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
1097				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
1098				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
1099				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
1100				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1101			})
1102		}
1103		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
1104			Harness:          actualHarness,
1105			Model:            actualModel,
1106			SessionID:        sessionID,

317				out.StoreErrOp = "CloseWithEvidence"
318				out.StoreErr = cerr
319				return out
320			}
321		case VerdictRequestChanges:
322			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
323				Kind:      "review",
324				Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
325				Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
326				Actor:     in.Assignee,
327				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
328				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
329			})
330			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
331			report.Detail = "pre-close review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
332			out.Approved = false
333		case VerdictBlock:
334			rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
335			classification := ClassifyReviewFindings(reviewRes)
336			if rationale == "" && len(classification.Evidence) == 0 {
337				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
338					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
339					Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
340					Body:      AppendEventSummary(ReviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
341					Actor:     in.Assignee,
342					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
343					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
344				})
345				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
346				report.Detail = "pre-close review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
347				report.ReviewRationale = ""
348				out.Report = report
349				out.Approved = false
350				return out
351			}
352			_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
353				Kind:      "review",
354				Summary:   "BLOCK",
355				Body:      AppendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
356				Actor:     in.Assignee,
357				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
358				CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
359			})
360			if classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
361				_ = in.Store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
362					Kind:      "review-malfunction",
363					Summary:   ReviewFindingClassMalfunction,
364					Body:      AppendEventSummary(classification.Reason, reviewSummary),
365					Actor:     in.Assignee,
366					Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
367					CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
368				})
369				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
370				report.Detail = "pre-close review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
371				out.Report = report
372				out.Approved = false
373				return out
374			}
375			if terminalClass := classifyTerminalReviewBlock(reviewRes); terminalClass != "" {
376				applyTerminalReviewBlock(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), terminalClass, report.ResultRev)
377				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock
378				report.Detail = "pre-close review: terminal " + terminalClass
379				out.Report = report
380				out.Approved = false
381				return out
382			}
383			// Non-terminal BLOCK: schedule one bounded repair cycle (review_fixable_gap).
384			// If a repair cycle has already been scheduled for this result_rev, fall
385			// through to the regular BLOCK triage path so the policy cannot loop.
386			if classification.AutomatedRepairEligible && !hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, report.ResultRev) {
387				groupID := ""
388				if reviewGroup != nil {
389					groupID = reviewGroup.Bundle.GroupID
390				}
391				repairCtx := scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair(
392					in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(),
393					groupID, report.ResultRev,
394					strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale),
395					report.ActualPower,
396				)
397				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap
398				report.Detail = "pre-close review: review_fixable_gap"
399				out.Report = report
400				out.Approved = false
401				out.RepairContext = repairCtx
402				return out
403			}
404			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
405			report.Detail = "pre-close review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
406			out.Approved = false
407		}
408		if reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictBlock || reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
409			_ = applyReviewTriageDecision(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), report.Tier)
410		}
411	
412		out.Report = report
413		return out
414	}
415	
416	func runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, reviewer BeadReviewer, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {
417		if reviewer == nil {
418			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: reviewer required")
419		}
420		if groupReviewer, ok := reviewer.(reviewGroupReviewer); ok {
421			return groupReviewer.ReviewGroup(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
422		}
423		result, err := reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
424		group := &ReviewGroupResult{
425			BeadID:    beadID,
426			ResultRev: resultRev,
427			Slots: []ReviewGroupSlotResult{
428				{
429					ReviewerIndex: 0,
430					Result:        result,
431				},
432			},
433		}
434		if err != nil {
435			group.Slots[0].Error = err.Error()
436		}
437		return group, err
438	}
439	
440	func reviewErrorReviewerIndex(group *ReviewGroupResult, res *ReviewResult) (int, bool) {
441		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) < 2 {
442			return 0, false
443		}
444		if res != nil {
445			for _, slot := range group.Slots {
446				if slot.Result == res {
447					return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
448				}
449			}
450			return res.ReviewerIndex, true
451		}
452		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
453			if slot.Error != "" {
454				return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
455			}
456		}
457		return 0, true
458	}
459	
460	func reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group *ReviewGroupResult) (*ReviewResult, error) {
461		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) == 0 {
462			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: no review slots")
463		}
464		var firstApprove *ReviewResult
465		var firstNonApprove *ReviewResult
466		var firstResult *ReviewResult
467		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
468			if slot.Result != nil && firstResult == nil {
469				firstResult = slot.Result
470			}
471			if slot.Error != "" && slot.Result == nil {
472				if firstResult != nil {
473					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
474				}
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
476			}
477			if slot.Result == nil {
478				if firstResult != nil {
479					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
480				}
481				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
482			}
483			res := slot.Result
484			switch res.Verdict {
485			case VerdictApprove:
486				if !reviewResultHasACEvidence(res) {
487					return res, fmt.Errorf("%w: approve verdict without per-AC evidence", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
488				}
489				if firstApprove == nil {
490					firstApprove = res
491				}
492			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
493				if firstNonApprove == nil {
494					firstNonApprove = res
495				}
496			default:
497				if firstNonApprove == nil {
498					firstNonApprove = res
499				}
500			}
501		}
502		if firstNonApprove != nil {
503			return firstNonApprove, nil
504		}
505		return firstApprove, nil
506	}
507	
508	func reviewResultHasACEvidence(res *ReviewResult) bool {
509		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictApprove {
510			return false
511		}
512		if len(res.PerAC) == 0 {
513			return false
514		}
515		for _, ac := range res.PerAC {
516			if strings.TrimSpace(ac.Evidence) == "" {
517				return false
518			}
519		}
520		return true
521	}
522	
523	// applyReviewTriageDecision runs the triage policy after a BLOCK or
524	// REQUEST_CHANGES review verdict has been recorded for `beadID`. It reads
525	// prior review events to compute the BLOCK count, biases the result toward
526	// re-attempt when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer
527	// (kind:review-pairing-degraded), then applies the chosen action by writing
528	// metadata, labels, and a kind:triage-decision event.
529	//
530	// Errors from the underlying store are best-effort: callers continue
531	// regardless so a triage-side failure cannot strand a bead in an
532	// inconsistent review state. This callout previously lived as a method on
533	// ExecuteBeadWorker; the C3
534	// extraction (ddx-a921ff01) moves it inside the post-merge-review pipeline
535	// driven by RunPostMergeReview / try.Attempt.
536	func applyReviewTriageDecision(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, currentTier string) error {
537		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
538		if err != nil {
539			return err
540		}
541	
542		var blockTimestamps []time.Time
543		var pairingDegraded []time.Time
544		for _, ev := range events {
545			switch {
546			case ev.Kind == "review" && strings.TrimSpace(ev.Summary) == "BLOCK":
547				blockTimestamps = append(blockTimestamps, ev.CreatedAt)
548			case ev.Kind == ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind:
549				pairingDegraded = append(pairingDegraded, ev.CreatedAt)
550			}
551		}
552	
553		// History excludes the current attempt. The just-appended BLOCK (if any)
554		// is the last entry in blockTimestamps and must not be counted as prior
555		// history per triage.Decide()'s contract.
556		historyCount := len(blockTimestamps)
557		if historyCount > 0 {
558			historyCount--
559		}
560		history := make([]triage.FailureMode, 0, historyCount)
561		for i := 0; i < historyCount; i++ {
562			history = append(history, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock)
563		}
564	
565		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
566		action := policy.Decide(beadID, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock, history)
567	
568		pairedDegraded := latestBlockPairedDegraded(blockTimestamps, pairingDegraded)
569		if pairedDegraded && action == triage.ActionEscalateTier {
570			action = triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext
571		}
572	
573		return applyTriageAction(store, beadID, actor, now, action, currentTier, pairedDegraded)
574	}
575	
576	// latestBlockPairedDegraded reports whether the most recent BLOCK event was
577	// paired with a review-pairing-degraded event from the same attempt window
578	// (between the previous BLOCK and this one).
579	func latestBlockPairedDegraded(blocks, pairing []time.Time) bool {
580		if len(blocks) == 0 || len(pairing) == 0 {
581			return false
582		}
583		latest := blocks[len(blocks)-1]
584		var prev time.Time
585		if len(blocks) >= 2 {
586			prev = blocks[len(blocks)-2]
587		}
588		for _, t := range pairing {
589			if t.After(prev) && !t.After(latest) {
590				return true
591			}
592		}
593		return false
594	}
595	
596	// applyTriageAction performs the side effects for a chosen Action: writes a
597	// tier-pin hint into bead.Extra (escalate_tier), moves the bead to proposed
598	// for operator-required outcomes, and always records a triage-decision event.
599	func applyTriageAction(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, action triage.Action, currentTier string, pairedDegraded bool) error {
600		body := map[string]any{
601			"action": string(action),
602			"mode":   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock),
603		}
604		if pairedDegraded {
605			body["pairing_degraded"] = true
606		}
607	
608		switch action {
609		case triage.ActionEscalateTier:
610			nextTier := nextEscalatedTier(currentTier)
611			body["tier_hint"] = string(nextTier)
612			_ = store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
613				if b.Extra == nil {
614					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
615				}
616				b.Extra[TriageTierHintKey] = string(nextTier)
617			})
618		case triage.ActionOperatorRequired:
619			if err := store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkPostReviewMalfunction, func(b *bead.Bead) {
620				if b.Extra == nil {
621					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
622				}
623				delete(b.Extra, TriageTierHintKey)
624				// Migration-only cleanup: defensive removal for legacy rows that escaped
625				// the lifecycle migration or arrived via external import.
626				b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
627				clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
628				bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
629					Reason:          "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung",
630					Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
631					Source:          "ddx agent execute-loop",
632					SuggestedAction: "review the blocked attempt and accept, split, block, or cancel",
633					Summary:         "review BLOCK triage requires operator decision",
634				})
635			}); err != nil {
636				return err
637			}
638		}
639	
640		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
641		return store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
642			Kind:      triageDecisionEventKind,
643			Summary:   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock) + ": " + string(action),
644			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
645			Actor:     actor,
646			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
647			CreatedAt: now,
648		})
649	}
650	
651	func clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b *bead.Bead) {
652		b.Owner = ""
653		if b.Extra == nil {
654			return
655		}
656		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-at")
657		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-pid")
658		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-machine")
659		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-session")
660		delete(b.Extra, "claimed-worktree")
661		delete(b.Extra, "execute-loop-heartbeat-at")
662	}
663	
664	// nextEscalatedTier returns the next tier above `current`. An unrecognised or
665	// empty current tier defaults to standard; smart is its own ceiling.
666	func nextEscalatedTier(current string) escalation.ModelTier {
667		switch escalation.ModelTier(current) {
668		case escalation.TierCheap:
669			return escalation.TierStandard
670		case escalation.TierStandard:
671			return escalation.TierSmart
672		case escalation.TierSmart:
673			return escalation.TierSmart
674		default:
675			return escalation.TierStandard
676		}
677	}

356	// TestPreCloseReview_CostCapFeedsReviewError verifies AC 1 + AC 3: when the
357	// drain-level cost cap is already exhausted before the pre-close review is
358	// dispatched, RunPostMergeReview must:
359	//   - NOT close the bead (Approved=false)
360	//   - append a review-error event with failure_class=cost_cap_exceeded
361	//   - classify it through the review_max_retries retry/exhaustion path
362	func TestPreCloseReview_CostCapFeedsReviewError(t *testing.T) {
363		store, first, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
364		const resultRev = "costcap-rev"
365	
366		// Build a cost cap tracker already past its limit so Tripped() is true.
367		capTracker := escalation.NewCostCapTracker(0.50, nil)
368		capTracker.Add("claude", 1.00) // spent $1.00 > max $0.50 → tripped
369	
370		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
371		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
372	
373		// The reviewer should NOT be called — the cap check must short-circuit.
374		var reviewerCalled bool
375		reviewer := beadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _ string, _ ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
376			reviewerCalled = true
377			return nil, errors.New("reviewer must not be called when cap is tripped")
378		})
379	
380		out := RunPostMergeReview(context.Background(), PostMergeReviewInput{
381			Bead: *first,
382			Report: ExecuteBeadReport{
383				BeadID:    first.ID,
384				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
385				SessionID: "sess-costcap",
386				ResultRev: resultRev,
387			},
388			Reviewer:      reviewer,
389			Store:         store,
390			ProjectRoot:   t.TempDir(),
391			Rcfg:          rcfg,
392			ReviewCostCap: capTracker,
393			Assignee:      "worker",
394			Now:           time.Now,
395		})
396	
397		require.False(t, out.Approved, "bead must not be closed when cost cap is exceeded")
398		assert.False(t, reviewerCalled, "reviewer must not be dispatched when cost cap is already tripped")
399	
400		events, err := store.Events(first.ID)
401		require.NoError(t, err)
402	
403		var reviewErrEvent *bead.BeadEvent
404		for i := range events {
405			if events[i].Kind == "review-error" {
406				reviewErrEvent = &events[i]
407				break
408			}
409		}
410		require.NotNil(t, reviewErrEvent,
411			"cost cap exceeded before dispatch must append a review-error event")
412		assert.Contains(t, reviewErrEvent.Body, "failure_class="+evidence.OutcomeReviewCostCapExceeded,
413			"review-error body must carry failure_class=cost_cap_exceeded")
414		assert.Contains(t, reviewErrEvent.Body, "result_rev="+resultRev,
415			"review-error body must carry the blocked result_rev")
416	
417		// AC 3: the bead must remain open (not closed by the cap refusal).
418		got, err := store.Get(first.ID)
419		require.NoError(t, err)
420		assert.NotEqual(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status,
421			"cost cap exceeded must not close the bead")
422	}

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":       true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":  true,
23		"land_conflict":          true,
24		"review_request_changes": true,
25		"review_block":           true,
26	}
27	
28	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
29	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
30	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
31	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
32		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
33	}
34	
35	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
36	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
37	// locking out a provider for too long.
38	//
39	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
40	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
41	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
42	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
43	
44	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
45	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
46		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
47		if probeResult != "" {
48			body += " probe=" + probeResult
49		}
50		if detail != "" {
51			body += "\n" + detail
52		}
53		return body
54	}
55	
56	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
57	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
58	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
59	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
60	
61	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
62	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
63	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
64	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
65	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
66		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
67		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
68		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
69		Status     string  `json:"status"`
70		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
71		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
72	}
73	
74	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
75	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
76	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
77	type EscalationSummary struct {
78		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
79		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
80		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
81		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
82	}
83	
84	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
85	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
86	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
87	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
88	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
89	// SuccessStatus.
90	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
91		out := EscalationSummary{
92			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
93			WinningTier:    winningTier,
94		}
95		if out.WinningTier == "" {
96			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
97		}
98		for _, a := range attempts {
99			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
100			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
101				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
102			}
103		}
104		return out
105	}
106	
107	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
108	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
109	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
110	type BeadEventAppender interface {
111		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
112	}
113	
114	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
115	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
116	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
117	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
118	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
119	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
120		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
121			return nil
122		}
123		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
124		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
125		if err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
129			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
130			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
131			Body:      string(body),
132			Actor:     actor,
133			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
134			CreatedAt: createdAt,
135		})
136	}

14	// ReviewGroup reviews the same bead/result_rev with two reviewer slots.
15	// The helper shares one evidence bundle and prompt file across both slots and
16	// returns the structured reviewer results alongside the shared bundle
17	// metadata. It does not change execute-loop close/block behavior.
18	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {

data.text:
All ACs satisfied. The implementation:

1. **`reviewerDispatchProfile`** now accepts `priorErrors int` and implements the three-rung ladder: `impl+1` (base), `impl+2` (one error), top-of-ladder (two+ errors).

2. **`countPriorReviewEscalationEvents`** counts both `review-error` and `review-pairing-degraded` events scoped by `result_rev` to determine escalation level.

3. **`kind:reviewer-escalated`** event is emitted before each escalated dispatch carrying `prior_error_count`, `new_min_power`, and `result_rev`.

4. **`reviewPairingDegradedBody`** now includes `result_rev` in its body so pairing-degraded events can be counted per result_rev.

5. **`ReviewerEventReader`** interface added to `DefaultBeadReviewer` (optional field; nil disables escalation while preserving base behavior).

6. The `MinPowerOverride` condition changed from `== 0` to `< reviewProfile.MinPower` so escalated floors correctly override the `BuildReviewExecuteRequest` base floor.
